skill.name: mcp-builder
skill.description: Guide for designing, implementing, and evaluating MCP servers for external APIs using Python FastMCP or the TypeScript MCP SDK.
skill.summary: Build MCP servers with agent-focused tools, validation, documentation, and evaluation workflows.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review found most weak-crypto, backtick, and URL hits are documentation examples or license text rather than malicious code. Real risk remains because the included evaluation harness can launch user-specified MCP server commands, connect to remote MCP endpoints with headers, pass environment variables, read or write evaluation files, and send tool outputs to Anthropic for scoring.

medium_findings.title: User-Specified Command Launch in Evaluation Harness
medium_findings.description: The evaluation harness accepts a command and argument list from the CLI, then passes them to the MCP stdio client. This is expected for local MCP testing, but running untrusted command values would execute local code under the user account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command path is directly user-controlled through CLI arguments and is used to create stdio server parameters. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, so the risk depends on whether users supply trusted server commands.

medium_findings.title: Remote MCP Connections With User-Supplied Headers
medium_findings.description: The harness supports SSE and streamable HTTP transports and accepts arbitrary URLs and headers. This can expose bearer tokens or other headers to a configured endpoint if users connect to an untrusted MCP server.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and documented, and headers are passed through to MCP clients. No malicious endpoint is hardcoded, but users must verify the endpoint before supplying credentials.

medium_findings.title: Tool Results Sent to Anthropic During Evaluation
medium_findings.description: The evaluation loop sends user questions, tool schemas, tool results, and conversation history to Anthropic Messages API calls. This is necessary for LLM-based evaluation, but sensitive tool outputs could leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly forwards messages and tool results to the Anthropic client. The privacy impact is real when evaluated MCP tools return confidential data, although the behavior is aligned with the stated evaluation function.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variables Passed to Child MCP Servers
medium_findings.description: The CLI accepts KEY=VALUE environment variables and forwards them into stdio MCP server execution. This is useful for test credentials, but it can expose secrets to any MCP server command selected by the user.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variables are parsed from user input and passed into the subprocess connection settings. This is a clear credential-handling risk if the target server is not trusted.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Backtick Hits Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Many high and medium static detections occur inside Markdown examples, XML examples, checklist text, or license wording. These files explain MCP server development and do not automatically execute those examples when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed the cited areas are fenced examples or instructional prose. There is no evidence that these Markdown snippets execute by themselves.

low_findings.title: Placeholder API Keys and Tokens in Examples
low_findings.description: Reference documentation includes placeholder API keys, bearer tokens, and GitHub token examples. These are not embedded real secrets, but marketplace users should treat them as examples and avoid copying real secrets into shared evaluation files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The strings are clearly placeholders in documentation and CLI examples. The pattern is safe as shipped, but it documents workflows that handle real credentials.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found in Reviewed Files
low_findings.description: Searches for explicit override, ignore-instructions, pre-approval, and administrator-authority phrases found no evidence of prompt injection attempts in the skill files reviewed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted phrase search across SKILL.md, reference files, scripts, and LICENSE.txt returned no matches for the required prompt-injection indicators.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess-Style MCP Stdio Startup
dangerous_patterns.description: The stdio connection path starts an MCP server from a user-provided command and arguments. This pattern should only be used with trusted local server commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in executable Python code and has direct local execution impact. It is part of the intended harness, not hidden behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Remote MCP Endpoint Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The SSE and HTTP connection paths accept user-provided URLs and optional headers. This can transmit headers or tool traffic to remote MCP endpoints selected at runtime.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and headers are controlled by CLI inputs and are passed into network MCP clients. The risk is contextual because no destination is hardcoded.

dangerous_patterns.title: LLM Evaluation Sends Tool Outputs Off-Process
dangerous_patterns.description: The harness forwards tool results back into Anthropic Messages API requests while evaluating tasks. Users should avoid evaluating tools that expose secrets or private records unless their data policy permits this.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow is visible in the message loop and uses the Anthropic client. The finding is highly relevant for privacy, but it supports the stated evaluation design.

content.value_statement: MCP server projects often fail because tools mirror APIs instead of supporting agent workflows. This skill guides design, implementation, testing, and evaluation for Python and TypeScript MCP servers.
actual_capabilities: Guides planning for agent-focused MCP tools and workflows.
Provides Python FastMCP implementation patterns with Pydantic validation.
Provides TypeScript MCP SDK patterns with Zod validation.
Includes reference guidance for naming, pagination, error handling, and response formats.
Includes a Python evaluation harness for stdio, SSE, and HTTP MCP servers.
Explains how to create XML evaluation questions with stable answers.
limitations: Does not generate a complete MCP server without user-specific API documentation.
Requires users to verify current MCP SDK documentation before implementation.
The evaluation harness requires Anthropic API access and compatible MCP server transports.
Running evaluations against untrusted servers can expose local commands, headers, or data.

use_cases.title: Design a New MCP Integration
use_cases.description: Plan tools, schemas, errors, pagination, and response formats before implementing an external API integration.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing MCP Tools
use_cases.description: Review an existing server for agent usability, context efficiency, validation quality, and clear tool descriptions.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Server Quality
use_cases.description: Create read-only evaluation questions and run an LLM-based harness to measure whether tools solve realistic tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an MCP Server
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan an MCP server for [service]. Identify the most valuable user workflows, proposed tools, required authentication, and response formats.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Core Tools
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a first Python or TypeScript implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Using this skill, implement the initial MCP server for [service] in [Python or TypeScript]. Include validation, error handling, pagination, and clear tool descriptions.

prompt_templates.title: Review Tool Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Audit an existing MCP server for agent usability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my MCP server for workflow coverage, naming, schemas, annotations, response size, and error messages. Suggest focused changes before adding new tools.

prompt_templates.title: Create and Run Evaluations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create ten read-only evaluation questions for this MCP server, verify the expected answers, then run the evaluation harness and summarize tool quality improvements.

output_examples.input: Plan an MCP server for a project management API.
output: A prioritized workflow map for projects, tasks, users, and comments.
A proposed tool list with read-only and write operation boundaries.
Validation, pagination, and error-handling notes for each core tool.

output_examples.input: Review my TypeScript MCP server implementation.
output: Findings about tool names, Zod schemas, annotations, response formats, and build configuration.
Concrete fixes for unclear descriptions, loose schemas, and oversized responses.

output_examples.input: Create evaluations for my GitHub MCP server.
output: Ten independent read-only questions with stable expected answers.
A recommendation to remove questions that require writes or time-sensitive data.

best_practices: Start with real user workflows, then design tools that complete those workflows with minimal calls.
Use strict input validation, clear annotations, bounded pagination, and concise default responses.
Evaluate only trusted MCP servers and avoid sending private tool results to external model APIs.
anti_patterns: Do not wrap every API endpoint as a separate tool without workflow design.
Do not run the evaluation harness with untrusted commands, URLs, headers, or environment variables.
Do not create evaluation questions that require destructive actions or unstable answers.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build MCP servers that expose external services through well-designed tools for AI agents.

faq.question: Does it support Python and TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Python FastMCP and the TypeScript MCP SDK.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python evaluation harness and connection helper scripts for MCP server testing.

faq.question: Is it safe to run evaluations on any MCP server?
faq.answer: No. Use trusted commands and endpoints because evaluations can pass credentials, headers, and tool outputs.

faq.question: Can it generate evaluation questions?
faq.answer: It explains how to create read-only XML evaluation questions with stable expected answers.

faq.question: Does it replace official MCP documentation?
faq.answer: No. It directs users to current MCP protocol and SDK documentation for implementation details.
